metadata.summary: Utility tools for GATE ARF simulations

documentation:
GARF = GATE ARF

```pip install garf```

Scripts associated with the publication:
Phys Med Biol. 2018 Oct 17;63(20):205013. doi: 10.1088/1361-6560/aae331.
Learning SPECT detector angular response function with neural network for accelerating Monte-Carlo simulations.
Sarrut D, Krah N, Badel JN, Létang JM.
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pubmed/30238925

A method to speed up Monte-Carlo simulations of single photon emission computed tomography (SPECT) imaging is proposed. It uses an artificial neural network (ANN) to learn the angular response function (ARF) of a collimator-detector system. The ANN is trained once from a complete simulation including the complete detector head with collimator, crystal, and digitization process. In the simulation, particle tracking inside the SPECT head is replaced by a plane. Photons are stopped at the plane, and the energy and direction are used as input to the ANN, which provides detection probabilities in each energy window. Compared to histogram-based ARF, the proposed method is less dependent on the statistics of the training data, provides similar simulation efficiency, and requires less training data. The implementation is available within the GATE platform.

- Gate10 test: https://github.com/OpenGATE/opengate/blob/master/opengate/tests/src/test043_garf.py

modules.name: garf.garf_model
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

classes.name: garf.garf_model.Net_v1
documentation:
Define the NN architecture (version 1)

Input:
- H:          nb of neurons per layer
- n_ene_win:  nb of energy windows

Description:
- simple Linear, fully connected NN
- two hidden layers
- Input X dimension is 3: angle1, angle2, energy
- Output Y dimension is n_ene_win (one-hot encoding)
- activation function is ReLu

modules.name: garf.garf_detector
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.load_nn
documentation:
Load a torch NN model + all associated info.
Always load the model on cpu only.
It should be moved to gpu only if needed.

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.normalize_logproba
documentation:
Convert un-normalized log probabilities to normalized ones (0-100%)
Not clear how to deal with exp overflow ?
(https://timvieira.github.io/blog/post/2014/02/11/exp-normalize-trick/)
noinspection PyUnreachableCode

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.remove_out_of_image_boundaries_numpy
documentation: Remove values out of the images (<0 or > size)

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.remove_out_of_image_boundaries_torch
documentation: Remove values out of the images (<0 or > size)

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.nn_predict_numpy
documentation:
Apply the NN to predict y from x
GPU vs CPU is managed by the "device" variable in the mode_data dic

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.compute_angle_offset_torch
documentation: compute the x,y offset according to the angle

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.compute_angle_offset_numpy
documentation: compute the x,y offset according to the angle

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.normalize_proba_with_russian_roulette
documentation: Consider rr times the values for the energy windows channel

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.image_from_coordinates_add_numpy
documentation:
Convert an array of pixel coordinates u,v (int) and corresponding weight
into an image
if hit_slice is True: the first slice stores all hits.
If not, the first slice is empty

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.arf_plane_intersection
documentation:
Project the x points (Ekine X Y Z dX dY dZ)
on the image plane defined by plane_U, plane_V, plane_center, plane_normal
-> See GarfDetectorPlane::intersection

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.arf_from_points_to_image_counts_OLD
documentation:
Input : position, direction on the detector plane, energy
Compute
- garf.nn_predict
- garf.compute_angle_offset
- garf.remove_out_of_image_boundaries2

Used in 1) GarfDetector class and 2) gate ARFActor

functions.name: garf.garf_detector.arf_from_points_to_image_counts
documentation:
Input : position, direction on the detector plane, energy
Compute
- garf.nn_predict
- garf.compute_angle_offset
- garf.remove_out_of_image_boundaries2

Used in 1) GarfDetector class and 2) gate ARFActor

methods.name: garf.garf_detector.GarfDetector.build_image_from_projected_points_torch
documentation:
Create an image from points projected on the ARF plane.
Parameters are:
- projected_batch = are the list of points projected on the plane
- self.output_image[i] is the current image to update

See : arf_from_points_to_image_counts
See : image_from_coordinates_add_numpy

methods.name: garf.garf_detector.GarfDetector.build_image_from_projected_points_numpy
documentation:
Create an image from points projected on the ARF plane.
Parameters are:
- projected_batch = are the list of points projected on the plane
- image is the current image to update

methods.name: garf.garf_detector.GarfDetector.nn_predict_torch
documentation: Apply the NN to predict y from x

modules.name: garf.garf_train
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: garf.garf_train.print_nn_params
documentation: Print parameters of neural network

functions.name: garf.garf_train.nn_prepare_data
documentation:
Prepare the data for training: normalisation (mean/std) and add information
in the model_data information structure.

functions.name: garf.garf_train.nn_get_optimiser
documentation: Create the optimize (Adam + scheduler)

functions.name: garf.garf_train.train_nn
documentation:
Train the ARF neural network.

x_train -- x samples (3 dimensions: theta, phi, E)
y_train -- output probabilities vector for N energy windows
params  -- dictionary of parameters and options

params contains:
- n_ene_win
- batch_size
- batch_per_epoch
- epoch_store_every
- H
- L
- epoch_max
- early_stopping
- gpu_mode : auto cpu gpu
